title: Machine-to-Machine with Client Credentials
description:
## A Backend Job Calling a Protected API

A scheduled **backend batch job** needs to call a protected API. There is **no user** involved — it runs on its own. Which OAuth 2.0 grant should it use to obtain an access token from Keycloak?

options.text: The **Client Credentials** grant — the confidential client (its service account) authenticates with its own credentials to get an access token.
The **Authorization Code** flow, redirecting a browser to Keycloak.
The **Resource Owner Password** grant using a shared user account's password.
No token is needed; backend jobs can call APIs anonymously.

explanation: When there is **no human user** and a service authenticates *as itself*, use the **Client Credentials** grant. A **confidential client** presents its own credentials (client ID + secret, or another client-auth method) to the token endpoint and receives an access token tied to its **service account**. Option **b** requires a browser and an interactive user — impossible for an unattended job. Option **c** (password grant) is discouraged and would mean managing a shared human account's password, which is poor practice and bypasses MFA/SSO. Option **d** is insecure — a protected API must require a valid token.

hints.title: No user, authenticate as the app
hints.body: If the question has no human in the loop, the client itself is the identity — that's Client Credentials with a service account.
